Last post covered type inference. This time, union types — when a variable can hold more than one type.
Last post mentioned that TypeScript still compiles even if you skip type annotations — but is that really the case? This post covers a really important TypeScript feature called type inference.
A lot of frontend devs around me run into TypeScript sooner or later, so here's a basic intro — starting with variables.
How Do You Write a Ternary Operator in CoffeeScript? (Spoiler: You Don't)

CoffeeScript doesn't support the usual JavaScript ternary operator syntax, so if you try the classic `a ? b : c` you'll just get an error. Here's a quick note on how to write the equivalent in CoffeeScript using if/then/else, including a couple of variations inside string interpolation.
CoffeeScript Event Not Firing? It Was the Parentheses All Along

While writing some CoffeeScript I ran into a weird one — my event handler just wouldn't work. I kept getting "TypeError: e is undefined" and then "ReferenceError: e is not defined." This is a quick note on what I tried and how I eventually fixed it.
CoffeeScript Basics: What This Compile-to-JS Language Actually Looks Like

A project might need me to write CoffeeScript, so I figured I'd poke at it a bit and figure out what this thing actually is. CoffeeScript isn't a brand-new language — it's a transpiled language that compiles down to JavaScript. This post walks through setting it up with Gulp and the basic syntax: variables, functions, objects, arrays, conditionals, and loops.
5 Ways to Flatten a Multidimensional Array in JavaScript (Including the ES2019 One)

I hit a task today where I needed to flatten a multidimensional array, so I'm jotting down what I found. This post walks through five different ways to do it — from the old concat tricks to reduce and spread — plus the shiny new ES2019 approach with flat(). A bonus recursive example is at the end too.
GitHub API Returns Garbled File Content? Here's How to Decode It

I was playing with the GitHub API the other day and one of its endpoints kept returning a wall of garbled text in the content field. This is a quick note on why that happens and how to decode it properly with base64 — including the two little gotchas that trip everyone up.
Hit a CORS Wall and Can't Touch the Backend? Wrap It With Google Apps Script

Calling an API from the frontend and getting slapped with a CORS error, but you can't change the backend? This post starts from the idea of the Same-origin policy, then uses Google Apps Script to wrap a little proxy around a third-party API. That turns it into your own same-origin API and gets you past the CORS restriction.
Rendering a Nested Array Inside an ES6 Template String? Use map, Not forEach

I hit a snag while writing some JavaScript today — I needed to render an array inside an ES6 Template String, but the obvious approach kept giving me undefined. Turns out the fix is to reach for map instead of forEach. Here's a quick note on what went wrong and how to do it properly.
